title: Method and apparatus for particle beam-induced processing of a defect of a microlithographic photomask
abstract: Method for particle beam-induced processing of a defect (D, D') of a microlithographic photomask (100), including the steps of: a) providing (S1) an image (300) of at least a portion of the photomask (100), b) determining (S2) a geometric shape of a defect (D, D') in the image (300) as a repair shape (302, 302'), with the repair shape (302, 302') comprising a number n of pixels (304), c) subdividing (S3), in computer-implemented fashion, the repair shape (302, 302') into a number k of sub-repair shapes (306), with an i-th of the k sub-repair shapes (306) having a number m, of pixels (304), which are a subset of the n pixels (304) of the repair shape (302, 302'), d) providing (S4) an activating particle beam (202) and a process gas at each of the m, pixels (304) of a first of the sub-repair shapes (306) for the purposes of processing the first of the sub-repair shapes (306), e) repeating (S5) step d) for the first of the sub-repair shapes (306) over a number j of repetition cycles, and f) repeating (S6) steps d) and e) for each further sub-repair shape (306).
claims:
Claims CLAIMS 1. Method for particle beam-induced processing of a defect (D, D') of a microlithographic photomask (100), including the steps of: a) providing (S1) an image (300) of at least a portion of the photomask (100), b) determining (S2) a geometric shape of a defect (D, D') in the image (300) as a repair shape (302, 302'), with the repair shape (302, 302') comprising a number n of pixels (304), c) subdividing (S3), in computer-implemented fashion, the repair shape (302, 302') into a number k of sub-repair shapes (306), with an i-th of the k sub- repair shapes (306) having a number m i of pixels (304), which are a subset of the n pixels (304) of the repair shape (302, 302'), d) providing (S4) an activating particle beam (202) and a process gas at each of the m i pixels (304) of a first of the sub-repair shapes (306) for the purposes of processing the first of the sub-repair shapes (306), e) repeating (S5) step d) for the first of the sub-repair shapes (306) over a number j of repetition cycles, and f) repeating (S6) steps d) and e) for each further sub-repair shape (306). 2. Method according to Claim 1, wherein the activating particle beam (202) and the process gas are solely provided at each of the m i pixels (304) of the first of the sub-repair shapes (306) in step d). 3. Method according to Claim 1 or 2, wherein the repair shape (302, 302') is subdivided in step c) into the number k of sub-repair shapes (306) on the basis of a threshold (W). 4. Method according to Claim 3, wherein the threshold (W) is an empirically determined value, which is determined before step a). 5. Method according to Claim 3 or 4, wherein the particle beam-induced processing comprises an etching of the defect (D, D') or a deposition of material on the defect (D, D') and the threshold (W) is determined from empirical values of 

an etching rate (R) or a deposition rate on the basis of a number n of pixels (304) of a repair shape (302, 302'). 6. Method according to any one of Claims 3 to 5, wherein the threshold (W) is an empirically determined value which is determined on the basis of parameters which are selected from a group comprising: the number n of pixels (304) of the repair shape (302, 302'), a size (a) of the pixels (304), an area of incidence (308) of the particle beam (202), a dwell time of the activating particle beam (202) on a respective pixel (304), a gas quantity flow rate with which the process gas is provided, a composition of the process gas and a gas quantity flow rate ratio of various gaseous components of the process gas. 7. Method according to any one of Claims 1 to 6, wherein the repair shape (302, 302') is subdivided into the plurality of sub-repair shapes (306) with the aid of a Voronoi approach. 8. Method according to Claim 7, wherein the sub-repair shapes (306) are determined as Voronoi regions starting from Voronoi centres (310) in step c), with each sub-repair shape (306) comprising the pixel (304) of the repair shape (302, 302') corresponding to the associated Voronoi centre (310) and all pixels (304) of the repair shape (302, 302') that are arranged closer to the associated Voronoi centre (310) than any other Voronoi centre (310) of the repair shape (302, 302'). 9. Method according to any one of Claims 1 to 8, wherein the repair shape (402) is subdivided into the plurality of sub-repair shapes (406) in such a way that the m"i pixels (410, 412) of a respective sub-repair shape (406) have the same distance from one another in a scanning direction (X). 10. Method according to any one of Claims 1 to 9, wherein the repair shape (502) comprises at least two spaced apart regions (504) and the repair shape (502) is subdivided into the plurality of sub-repair shapes (506) in such a way that each sub-repair shape (506) comprises at most one of the at least two spaced apart regions (504). 11. Method according to any one of Claims 1 to 10, wherein said method comprises the following step before step d): calculating a sequence in which the activating particle beam (202) is successively provided at the m i pixels (304) of the first of the sub-repair shapes (306) such that a depletion of the process gas by way of a chemical reaction activated by the activating particle beam (202) is implemented uniformly over the sub-repair shape (306). 12. Method according to any one of Claims 1 to 11, wherein the sequence in which steps d) and e) are carried out in step f) for the further sub-repair shapes (306) differs from a row-by-row and/or column-by-column sequence and/or is randomly distributed. 13. Method according to any one of Claims 1 to 12, wherein the repair shape (302, 302') is subdivided in a number h of mutually different subdivisions (312, 316) into sub-repair shapes (306, 306') in step c), and steps d) to f) are carried out for each of the h subdivisions (312, 316). 14. Method according to Claim 13, wherein steps d) to f) are carried out for each of the h subdivisions (312, 316) over a number g of repetition cycles, where g is less than j, and/or over a number j/h of repetition cycles. 15. Method according to Claim 13 or 14, wherein the number h of subdivisions (312, 316) differ from one another by way of a displacement, in particular a lateral displacement, of boundaries (318) of their sub-repair shapes (306) relative to the repair shape (302, 302'). 16. Method according to any one of Claims 1 to 15, wherein steps d) to f) are repeated over a number p of repetition cycles, and wherein p is an integer greater than or equal to two. 17. Apparatus (200) for particle beam-induced processing of a defect (D, D') of a microlithographic photomask (100), comprising: means (210) for providing an image (300) of at least a portion of a photomask (100), 

 a computing apparatus (226) for determining a geometric shape of a defect (D, D') in the image (300) as a repair shape (302, 302'), with the repair shape (302, 302') comprising a number n of pixels (304) and being configured to subdivide the repair shape (302, 302') into a plurality of sub-repair shapes (306) in computer-implemented fashion, and means (210, 220) for providing an activating particle beam and a process gas at each pixel (304) of every sub-repair shape (306) over a number j of repetition cycles for processing the respective sub-repair shape (306). 18. Computer program product comprising instructions which, when executed by a computing apparatus (226) for controlling an apparatus (200) for particle beam-induced processing of a defect of a microlithographic photomask, prompt the apparatus (200) to carry out the method steps according to any one of Claims 1 to 16. 19. Method for determining a threshold (W) for subdividing a repair shape (302, 302') on the basis of the threshold (W) into a number k of sub-repair shapes (306) during particle beam-induced processing of a defect (D, D') of a microlithographic photomask (100), including the steps of i) particle beam-induced processing (S1') of a first test defect (606) of a photomask (100) using predetermined processing parameters, the first test defect (606) having a first size (G 3 ), ii) determining (S2') a quality of the processing of the first test defect (606), iii) repeating (S3') steps i) and ii) for modified processing parameters until processing parameters are determined, for which the determined quality is better than or equal to a predetermined quality, iv) particle beam-induced processing (S4') of further test defects (602, 604, 608, 610) of the photomask (100) using the determined processing parameters, with the further test defects (602, 604, 608, 610) each having a size (G 1 , G 2 , G 4 , G 5 ) that differs from the sizes of the other further test defects and from the size (G 3 ) of the first test defect (606), v) determining (S5') the quality of the processing for each further test defect (602, 604, 608, 610), and vi) determining (S6') the threshold (W) on the basis of the quality determined for the first and the further test defects (602, 604, 606, 608, 610).
